does: This paper develops a video-model-based evaluation system for robotic policies using Veo, a frontier video foundation model. The system generates diverse simulated robot interactions by conditioning on robot actions and maintaining multi-view consistency across generated frames. The authors optimize Veo for robotics use cases through action conditioning and integrate generative image-editing and multi-view completion techniques to synthesize realistic variations of real-world scenes. The evaluation framework spans nominal performance assessment, out-of-distribution generalization testing, and safety probing (physical and semantic), enabling comprehensive policy evaluation beyond the in-distribution scenarios typically studied in prior work.
connects: This work builds on the emerging use of generative video models for robotics simulation, extending beyond prior limited applications that focused primarily on in-distribution evaluation. It responds to the challenge of scaling robot policy evaluation, a problem previously addressed through expensive real-world testing or limited domain-specific simulators. The research combines recent advances in video foundation models (particularly large-scale generative models like Veo) with established robotics evaluation methodologies and multi-view synthesis techniques. It draws on work in generative world models, visuomotor policy learning, and domain randomization for OOD generalization, while incorporating principles from generative image editing and 3D-consistent view synthesis to create realistic, varied evaluation scenarios.
enables: This system opens pathways for scalable, flexible robot policy evaluation without expensive real-world deployment or task-specific simulator engineering. It enables rapid iteration on safety testing by generating diverse edge cases and OOD scenarios synthetically, potentially accelerating the development cycle for safe robotic systems. The approach points toward automated, generative evaluation frameworks that could support continuous assessment of policies across semantic and physical variations. Future work could integrate this evaluation capability into closed-loop policy improvement pipelines, combine multiple video models for complementary coverage, or extend the framework to complex multi-agent scenarios and long-horizon reasoning tasks.
